mcqs.question: In horizontal microprogramming, the control word typically contains:

options.text: One bit for each micro-operation, allowing parallel activation of multiple control signals
Encoded bits that require decoding to generate control signals
Only the address of the next microinstruction
A single bit indicating the type of instruction being executed

mcqs.explanation: Horizontal microprogramming uses a long control word where each bit directly controls a specific control signal, enabling parallel activation of multiple signals for faster execution.

mcqs.question: Vertical microprogramming differs from horizontal microprogramming primarily in:

options.text: The use of a control memory for storing microinstructions
The encoded format of control words, requiring additional decoding circuitry
The sequential execution of microinstructions without branching
The use of read-only memory instead of random-access memory

mcqs.explanation: Vertical microprogramming uses encoded control words to reduce the width of the control memory, requiring decoders to generate the actual control signals, which adds hardware complexity but reduces memory width.

mcqs.question: Consider a CPU with 50 control signals. How many bits are required in a horizontal microinstruction control word compared to a 5-way vertical microinstruction (using 3-bit fields)?

options.text: Horizontal: 50 bits; Vertical: 15 bits
Horizontal: 50 bits; Vertical: 10 bits
Horizontal: 100 bits; Vertical: 25 bits
Horizontal: 25 bits; Vertical: 50 bits

mcqs.explanation: Horizontal microprogramming requires one bit per control signal (50 bits), while vertical encoding with 5 fields of 3 bits each requires 15 bits, significantly reducing control memory width.

mcqs.question: A Programmable Logic Array (PLA) used in hardwired control units implements:

options.text: Sequential logic for instruction sequencing and microprogram counter management
Combinational logic to generate control signals from instruction opcodes and timing states
Read-only memory for storing microinstructions
Random-access memory for temporary storage of intermediate control signals

mcqs.explanation: A PLA implements combinational logic using AND and OR gate arrays to generate control signals based on the current instruction opcode and timing/sequencing inputs, forming the core of hardwired control.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of hardwired control over microprogrammed control?

options.text: Faster execution time due to direct hardware signal generation
Easier to modify or extend the instruction set
More suitable for RISC architectures requiring high-speed operation
Lower cost for simple instruction sets with few instructions

mcqs.explanation: Hardwired control is inflexible and difficult to modify once implemented, making it harder to extend the instruction set compared to microprogrammed control, which can be easily reprogrammed.

mcqs.question: Which control unit design is more commonly associated with modern RISC processors and why?

options.text: Microprogrammed control, because RISC instructions are complex and require flexible implementation
Hardwired control, because RISC emphasizes simple, fixed-format instructions executed in single cycles, requiring fast signal generation
PLA-based control, because PLAs can implement complex instruction decoding efficiently
Horizontal microprogramming, because it allows parallel execution of multiple RISC operations

mcqs.explanation: RISC processors typically use hardwired control because the simple, fixed-format instructions can be decoded quickly using combinational logic, providing the high-speed operation essential for pipelined RISC architectures.

mcqs.question: In a microprogrammed control unit, the timing overhead for executing a microinstruction includes: (i) fetching the microinstruction from control memory, (ii) decoding the microinstruction, (iii) generating control signals. If the control memory access time is 20ns and signal generation takes 10ns, what is the minimum microinstruction cycle time?

options.text: 10ns
20ns
30ns
40ns

mcqs.explanation: The minimum cycle time must accommodate both fetching the microinstruction (20ns) and generating control signals (10ns), which typically occur in sequence, requiring at least 30ns for one microinstruction cycle.

mcqs.question: A processor designer must choose between hardwired and microprogrammed control for a new CPU that supports 150 instructions, with 40% being complex multi-cycle instructions. The target market requires frequent firmware updates to add proprietary extensions. Which control approach should be chosen and what is the primary reason?

options.text: Hardwired control, because it provides lower latency for complex multi-cycle instructions
Microprogrammed control, because the flexibility to update firmware allows adding new instructions without hardware redesign
PLA-based control, because it offers a balanced compromise between speed and flexibility
Horizontal microprogramming, because it provides the fastest execution for all instruction types

mcqs.explanation: Microprogrammed control is preferred when firmware updates and instruction set extensions are frequently required, as the control logic can be modified by changing the microprogram in control memory without hardware redesign.
